description_long: This hammer features a robust, metallic head with a pointed tip and a flat striking surface, suggesting a specialized function beyond standard carpentry. The head is mounted on a long, cylindrical handle that appears to be reinforced with a metallic or composite shaft, and the base of the handle is capped with a wide, stable end. The head includes several mechanical components, such as bolts and springs, giving it an industrial or possibly pneumatic appearance. The color palette is primarily metallic gray, black, and brown, with some lighter accents and visible mechanical details. The overall design is utilitarian and engineered for heavy-duty use, likely in construction or urban maintenance settings. The hammer appears well-constructed, with no visible signs of wear or branding.
description: A heavy-duty hammer with a mechanical, metallic head and a reinforced cylindrical handle, featuring bolts and springs for industrial use. The design is robust and engineered for demanding urban tasks.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed tip of the hammer head, the mechanical components, and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the layered structure of the head, visible bolts, and the spring mechanism, highlighting the industrial design.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the flat back of the hammer head and the symmetrical handle, with the base cap clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the mechanical details and the robust connection between head and handle.
attribute_handle_type: straight cylindrical handle with a metallic or composite shaft and a wide, stable metallic end cap; surface appears smooth with minimal texturing for grip.
attribute_head_type: specialized industrial hammer head with a pointed tip and flat striking face, featuring mechanical components such as bolts and a spring mechanism; not a standard claw or ball-peen type.
